topic.title: DS20 Badge: Polled IIC Completion (Diagnostic and Resolution)

header.para: DS20 Badge: Polled IIC Completion (Diagnostic and Resolution)

body.para: A DS20 cold boot mis-badges itself as "AlphaPC 264DP" (SYSTYPE member 1) instead of "AlphaServer DS20" (member 6). The cause is a missing device-completion event, but it is delivered by a polled kernel process, not an interrupt. This topic supersedes the earlier interrupt-completion topic: it records the source-proven causal chain, the PCF8584 PIN handshake the polled driver depends on, proof that the EmulatR controller model already completes the transfer correctly, the one open question and how to settle it, and the resolution. The same missing element gates a whole class of device waits, so this is broader than the badge text.
Symptom
HWRPB scan reports SYSTYPE=0x22 (DEC_TSUNAMI) and SYSVAR=0x405. Member = (SYSVAR >> 10) & 0x3F = 1, which the SRM banners as "AlphaPC 264DP". Member 6 would banner "AlphaServer DS20".
Correction: the interrupt theory is disproven

A prior theory held that EmulatR never raised the PCF8584 transfer-completion interrupt the SRM IIC driver waits on. It is wrong on this platform. The shipped V7.3-2 DS20 firmware runs the IIC driver polled: iic_driver.c:138 defines #define POLLED (MIKASA || ALCOR || RAWHIDE || PC264 || K2 || TAKARA), and PC264 is the DS20 build. In iic_init the #if POLLED arm is compiled in; the #else interrupt arm (int_vector_set(IIC_VECTOR, iic_service, ...), int_flag = IIC_ENI, mode = DDB$K_INTERRUPT) is compiled out. Measurement agrees: across the entire boot, ENI (control bit 0x08) is never written. The iic_service ISR at SCB vectors 0xa9/0xaa and the Cchip DRIR bit 48 routing are therefore inert here, and the EMULATR_IIC_IRQ_BIT sweep was correctly observed to do nothing.
Proven causal chain (polled)

1. The badge is a device-open result. get_sysvar() (pc264.c:636) returns member 6 if fopen("iic_ocp0","sr+") succeeds, member 8 if fopen("iic_8574_ocp") succeeds, else member 1. Member 1 means both opens returned NULL. build_hwrpb ORs member << 10 into the base SYSVAR 0x5 and stores it; member 1 yields 0x405.

2. The open misses because the node was never registered. iic_ocp0 is a static node-table entry at IIC node 0x40, type IIC_LED_TYPE, test = 1 (iic_driver.c:225). iic_init creates its inode only if the verify read iic_rw_common(0x40, 1, buf, IIC_READ_DIR) returns rec_count == 1 (the loop guard if (status != 1) continue;, iic_driver.c:1076).

3. Completion is delivered by a poll process, not an interrupt. Under #if POLLED, iic_init does krn$_create(iic_poll_rt, ... "srom_poll" ...) (iic_driver.c:1031). That process loops: read S1; if (status.bits.pin == 0) iic_service(pb); (iic_driver.c:1247-1252). iic_service advances the transfer and, on completion, does krn$_post(&pb->misr_t.sem, IIC_SR_DONE).

4. The verify blocks on that semaphore with a 2-second timeout. iic_rw_common stages the address, writes IIC_START | int_flag (int_flag = 0 when polled), starts IIC_MAS_TIMEOUT (2 s), and status = krn$_wait(&pb->misr_t.sem) (iic_driver.c:587-594). If the poll process never posts the semaphore, the timer wins: TIMEOUT$K_SEM_VAL -> pb->rec_count = 0 (iic_driver.c:614) -> node not registered -> fopen("iic_ocp0") misses -> member 1 -> "AlphaPC 264DP".
The PCF8584 PIN handshake the poll loop depends on

PIN ("Pending Interrupt Not", MSB of status register S1) is the synchronization flag the polled driver reads. Per the NXP/Philips PCF8584 datasheet (sec 6.8.2): PIN = 0 (active) means the controller has completed a byte and requires servicing; PIN = 1 (inactive) means idle/busy-not-ready. The driver tests PIN to know when a byte transfer/reception is complete.

table.style: border-width:0px; border-spacing:0px; border-collapse:collapse; cell-border-width:1px; border-color:#000000; border-style:solid; head-row-background-color:#00ffff; alt-row-background-color:none;

td.para: START issued (STA set), address clocked out
One byte transmitted/received (9 clocks incl. ACK)
PIN -> 1 (inactive) until the next byte arrives
All status bits reset to 0 (software reset)

para.text: When ENI is also set, the same PIN = 0 edge additionally drives the external INT output. On the polled DS20 build ENI is never set, so PIN is read as a pure status flag.

body.para: The controller model already completes the read

EmulatR's IicPcf8584 presents PIN = 0 (status 0x00) on an ACK'd address (startTransaction), serves a P2 dummy on the first S0 read, and on an IIC_NACK control write in the read phase shows LRB = 1 (status 0x08, P3). Tracing a 1-byte node-0x40 read (count = 1, rec_count = -1) through iic_service against this model completes in exactly three service calls:
call 1 (rec_count -1 -> 0): status 0x00 ok; read S0 = dummy; buffer[0] staged
call 2 (rec_count 0): status 0x00; read S0 = real byte; rec_count -> 1;
count == rec_count -> write IIC_NACK -> model status -> 0x08
call 3 (rec_count 1): status 0x08 (not an error); count == rec_count ->
write IIC_STOP; krn$_post(misr_t.sem, IIC_SR_DONE)
iic_rw_common then returns pb->rec_count == 1, so iic_init would register iic_ocp0 and the DS20 would badge correctly. The controller model is therefore not the defect: it is ready to be polled.
The open question: why the poll process is not serviced

Because the model would complete the read if driven, the live failure is that iic_service is not called during iic_init's early verify (the verify runs at roughly cycle 185M, immediately after the krn$_create of srom_poll). The IIC-armed capture supports this: the waiter spins on a DRAM semaphore word with only about three controller MMIO touches in the window -- if iic_poll_rt were alive its while(1) would flood the window with S1 reads. Two candidates remain, and they must be measured, not assumed:

(A) Scheduler/dispatch gap. The just-created srom_poll process is not dispatched by the SRM cooperative scheduler in time to service the verify, so the 2 s timeout always wins. This is the broad-implications case: every krn$_create'd poll/worker process plus krn$_wait-on-completion driver would fail the same way at init time.

(B) Controller PIN-presentation gap at this node. The poll process runs but its S1 read does not see PIN == 0 for node 0x40 at that moment (e.g. an iic_fp1 vs iic_fp2 routing or phase detail), so iic_service is never entered. This is IIC-model-local.
Diagnostic procedure
Build RelWithDebInfo with -DEMULATR_TRACE_HOOKS=ON, arm the retire window on the first node-0x40 START, and read off whether the poll process executes:
unset EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_PA
export EMULATR_TRACE_WINDOW=1
export EMULATR_RETIRE_TRACE_DIR=./traces
export EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_ON_IIC=0x40
export EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_INSTRS=4000000
export EMULATR_GMEM_WATCH=0x2058
export EMULATR_IIC_TRACE=1
export EMULATR_FLASH_ROM=ds20_flash.rom; rm -f ds20_flash.rom
unset EMULATR_PLATFORM
./run_fw.sh ds20 cold 2>&1 | tee fw_ds20_iicpoll.out

Decision: count S1 (status) reads in the window and check whether the retired PC ever lands at iic_poll_rt's entry. A continuous stream of S1 reads, or PC at iic_poll_rt, points to (B) -- then inspect the PIN value the model returns for node 0x40. Essentially no S1 reads, and no dispatch of srom_poll, points to (A) -- the cooperative scheduler is not running the created process; confirm by checking whether the krn$_create for "srom_poll" returns and whether the dispatcher ever selects it. Do not sweep EMULATR_IIC_IRQ_BIT; the interrupt path is compiled out and any bit is inert.
Resolution

The fix is to deliver the polled completion: ensure iic_poll_rt is dispatched and reaches iic_service for node 0x40 during iic_init, so the verify returns rec_count == 1, the node registers, and the same deterministic firmware path badges member 6 "AlphaServer DS20" with no other change. If the measurement lands on (A), the work is at the cooperative-scheduler / krn$_create dispatch layer (which also unblocks the device class below) -- not in the IIC model. If it lands on (B), it is a localized fix in IicPcf8584's PIN presentation for the verify. No SSOT change either way; interruptPending() and EMULATR_IIC_IRQ_BIT remain in the tree, default-off and inert on PC264, for the WEBBRICK-class interrupt path only.
Broader implications

The krn$_create worker plus krn$_wait-on-a-posted-semaphore plus 2 s timeout-fallback idiom is the SRM's standard device-completion pattern, shared by the floppy controller (the F3/F4/F5 recalibrate/seek timeouts) and IDE/ATAPI (the post-dva0 stall). If the completion is not delivered, each driver times out and the firmware takes its degraded fallback. The badge is the most visible instance of a general "block, then fall back" failure mode; resolving the completion delivery here is expected to clear the class.

See Also. DS20 Platform Identity: The IIC Completion Interrupt and the "AlphaPC 264DP" Badge [SUPERSEDED]; Diagnostics: GuestMemory Store-Watch and Retire-Trace Arm; HWRPB Key/Value Offset Map; Virtualization Methods.
